title: podman machine init - allow for overriding ignition file
body:
Given the newest "container on the desktop" news it would be great to have podman in a good usable state on other platforms besides Linux. Currently on my work machine we're MITM with CA certificates. On a `podman machine create` I can use my own ignition and do the configuration podman does by default such as setting up a user and ssh-keys and provide my corps CA certificates. This can be somewhat cumbersome providing this to my co-worker developers. 

It would be great if we can provide an ignition file with the CA certificates etc and `podman machine create` would merge in it's existing configuration. Without these CA certs on my work machine I cannot access the outside net or do pulls.

/kind-feature

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@baude @ashley-cui Did you get a chance to work on this?

@anthr76 how would you feel about expanding the command line with something like `--ign-file` but instead of a merge, it would be the complete ignition file.  I ask because I'm a little worried that merging may be more difficult, esp when merging different sections here and there.  What do you think?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@ashley-cui @baude I think overriding the ignition file is the best path forward.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@baude @ashley-cui Did you get a chance to look at this?
